revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ckb:فوتاگۆ کۆفون]][[en:Futago Kofun]][[ja:二子古墳]][[zh:二子古坟]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Anjō, Tōkai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Futago Kofun | native_name =二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 160312 Whole view of Futago Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Futago Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Futago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.928888888889|N|137.09666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Anjo|qid=Q270806|1=Anjō}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 3rd to early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Futago Kofun'''|二子古墳|Futago Kofun}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Sakurai neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Anjō, Aichi|Anjō, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, and its borders were expanded in 1955.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172225l|title=二子古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Futago Kofun is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), located in the on the Hekikai Plateau in {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|qid=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River|1=Yahagi River}} basin in western Mikawa. It is the second largest in the prefecture after the [[Shōbōji Kofun]] in [[:en:Nishio, Aichi|Nishio]], and the largest of the Sakurai Kofun Group, which also includes the {{ill|Himeogawa Kofun|en|Himeogawa Kofun|qid=Q85766592|lt=Himeogawa Kofun|1=Himeogawa Kofun}} and twenty other tombs. The tumulus has a total length of 81 meters. The anterior portion has a width of 36 meters and height of 6.7 meters and the posterior portion has a width of 45 meters and height of 10 meters. The summit is flat, and there is a square protrusion about 15 meters square on the east side of the anterior portion, which may have been used as a stage for ceremonial purposes. What appears to be a space for an altar is still in its original form at the west foot. The tumulus is estimated to be around the last half of the 3rd to the first half of the 4th century due to the lack of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]''. Remnants of a moat remain along its western side.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Futago Kofun first appears in academic literature in 1887, at which time it was crowned by the small Sakurai Tenjin Jinja {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1936 and again in 1959 and 1990. In 2003, the Anjō City Board of Education conducted a survey by excavating a total of five trenches. This excavation confirmed the presence of a circumferential moat ten meters wide and one meter deep on the north side of the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been excavated, but only some fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.
The site is about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Horiuchikōen Station|en|Horiuchikōen Station|qid=Q5903325|lt=Horiuchikōen Station|1=Horiuchikōen Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Nishio Line|en|Meitetsu Nishio Line|qid=Q6810417|lt=Meitetsu Nishio Line|1=Meitetsu Nishio Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|qid=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
*[https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/syubetsu/siseki.html Aichi Cultural Properties Navi] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://ansyobunka.jp/rekihaku/collection/kohungun/hutago/kfn-hutago.html Anjo City Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}}
Futago Kofun<
